title: pseudognaphalium luteoalbum subsp. affine
intro: Pseudognaphalium luteoalbum subsp. affine, also known as California everlasting or bush sunflower, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Baja California. It is a shrub-like perennial plant with yellow daisy-like flowers that has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and possessing antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Pseudognaphalium luteoalbum subsp. affine include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat various skin conditions, wounds, and minor infections.
